perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Delaware, without regard to its principles of conflicts of law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of Delaware, and any disputes arising from or related to this agreement shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The decision of the arbitrator shall be final and binding on both parties, and judgment upon the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court having jurisdiction thereof. Each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees in connection with any arbitration proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: By mandating binding arbitration and stipulating that each party bears their own costs, the contract potentially removes the consumer's right to pursue legal action in court and recover attorney's fees, which may be available under Delaware consumer protection laws, particularly in cases of fraud or misrepresentation related to the confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: 6 Del. C. § 2513 allows for private causes of action for consumer fraud and the potential recovery of attorney's fees. The modified clause, by forcing arbitration and preventing recovery of attorney fees, limits the consumer's ability to fully exercise these rights, thus contradicting the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 > Chapter 25 Authenticated PDF Â§ 2501 Â§ 2502 Â§ 2503 Â§ 2504 Â§ 2505 Â§ 2506 Â§ 2507 Â§ 2508 Â§ 2509 Â§ 2510 TITLE 6 Commerce and Trade SUBTITLE II Other Laws Relating to Commerce and Trade CHAPTER 25. Prohibited Trade Practices Subchapter I. General Provisions Â§ 2501. Falsely advertising goods as property of an insolvent or as damaged. No person engaged in the sale of any goods, wares or merchandise within the State shall publicly and falsely, and with intent to deceive the general buying public, advertise or otherwise represent that the goods, wares or merchandise are or were either in whole or in part the property of any insolvent or bankrupt or the assignee of any insolvent or bankrupt, or that such goods, wares or merchandise were either in whole or in part damaged by fire or accident of any kind. 18 Del. Laws, c. 661 ;Â 20 Del. Laws, c. 70 ;Â 20 Del. Laws, c. 571 ;Â 26 Del. Laws, c. 207 ;Â Code 1915, Â§ â3580;Â Code 1935, Â§ â4074;Â 6 Del. C. 1953, Â§ â2501;Â Â§ 2502. Sale of goods in Wilmington as a removed stock or in other than regular business manner. No person shall engage in the sale of any goods, wares or merchandise, within the corporate limits of the City of Wilmington, which have been brought to that city or consigned to any person in that city, for the purpose of the special sale thereof, as a removed stock of goods, or for the purpose of any sale thereof in any other than a regular business manner, by regular established merchants of that city. This section shall not prevent any person from embarking in the regular business of a merchant by any other method than those herein or elsewhere in the laws of this State prohibited, and this section shall not apply to goods and chattels shipped to Wilmington from other points of the State. 18 Del. Laws, c. 661 ;Â 20 Del. Laws, c. 70 ;Â 20 Del. Laws, c. 571 ;Â 26 Del. Laws, c. 207 ;Â Code 1915, Â§ â3580;Â Code 1935, Â§ â4074;Â 6 Del. C. 1953, Â§ â2502;Â Â§ 2503. Penalties; presumption of int
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a binding arbitration clause that prevents consumers from pursuing legal action in court and potentially recovering attorney's fees. The Law Explanation states that 6 Del. C. § 2513 allows for private causes of action for consumer fraud and the potential recovery of attorney's fees. The original text did not have this provision. The Omission Legal Contradiction definition states that a legal contradiction happens when a contract omits a legally mandated consumer protection, making it non-compliant. The scraped law snippet 1 only contains general provisions regarding falsely advertising goods; therefore, it is not helpful. The modified text contradicts the law by removing the consumer's ability to fully exercise their rights by forcing arbitration and preventing recovery of attorney fees.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that a breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which money damages alone could be an inadequate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief only.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the remedy to injunctive relief, the clause removes the possibility of seeking monetary damages for actual losses incurred as a result of the breach. This could be a violation of Delaware contract law, which generally allows for the recovery of compensatory damages in breach of contract cases.
perturbation.law_citation: JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. v. Data Payment Sys., Inc., 2014 WL 495676, at *13 (Del. Super. Ct. Jan. 29, 2014)
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law provides for compensatory damages to make the non-breaching party whole. By restricting remedies to injunctive relief only, the clause contradicts this general principle of Delaware contract law, potentially preventing the recovery of actual losses.

We hope you'll learn more about us, and help support our work. What's New? State Regulations LII now publishes state regulations for all 50 U.S. states . Updates are released quarterly. We're hiring! perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts established legal principles by limiting remedies to only injunctive relief, which is a violation under Delaware contract law. The original text acknowledges that a breach may cause irreparable harm not adequately covered by money alone; the changed text removes the possibility of monetary damages entirely. The 'Law Explanation' supports this, stating that 'Delaware law provides for compensatory damages to make the non-breaching party whole. By restricting remedies to injunctive relief only, the clause contradicts this general principle of Delaware contract law, potentially preventing the recovery of actual losses.' The scraped snippets do not directly address the specifics of Delaware contract law, but the absence of a snippet negating the 'Law Explanation' further supports that a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party. Each Party hereto shall not be obligated to enter into any further agreement with the other except as such party in its sole judgment may deem advisable. Neither Party shall be obligated to compensate the other for exchanging any information under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party. Each Party hereto shall not be obligated to enter into any further agreement with the other except as such party in its sole judgment may deem advisable.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Neither Party shall be obligated to compensate the other for exchanging any information under this Agreement.', the document appears to indicate that either party *could* be compensated, but does not specify compensation amounts and conditions. This is especially troublesome because the document involves potential patent acquisition opportunities. In the absence of an explicit statement about compensation, Delaware's Trade Secret law could apply, creating a legal contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware's Trade Secret law can mandate reasonable royalty payments in cases of misappropriation. Omitting the clear statement that no compensation is required introduces ambiguity and could potentially expose the Receiving Party to claims for compensation under trade secret law, even if that was not the intent of the parties.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 6 Authenticated PDF Â§ 2001 Â§ 2002 Â§ 2003 Â§ 2004 Â§ 2005 Â§ 2006 Â§ 2007 Â§ 2008 Â§ 2009 TITLE 6 Commerce and Trade SUBTITLE II Other Laws Relating to Commerce and Trade CHAPTER 20. Trade Secrets Â§ 2001. Definitions. The following words, terms and phrases, when used in this chapter, shall have the meanings ascribed to them in this section, except where the context clearly indicates a different meaning: (1) âImproper meansâ shall include theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. (2) âMisappropriationâ shall mean: a. Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or b. Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: 1. Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or 2. At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that his or her knowledge of the trade was: A. Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; B. Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or C. Derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or 3. Before a material change of the personâs position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. (3) âPersonâ shall mean a natural person, corporation, statutory trust, business trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or agency, or any other legal or commercial entity. (4) âTrade secretâ shall mean information, including a formula, pattern, compilation, program, device, method, technique or process, that: a. Derives independent economic value, actual or potential, from not being generally known to, and not b
perturbation.justification: The omission of the statement 'Neither Party shall be obligated to compensate the other for exchanging any information under this Agreement' creates a potential legal contradiction with Delaware's Trade Secret law. Snippet 1 defines 'Misappropriation' as the 'Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent'. By removing the explicit statement of no compensation, the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding compensation for the use of confidential information, potentially opening the door to claims of misappropriation under Delaware law if a trade secret is involved and used without clear compensation terms. The original text explicitly negated any obligation for compensation, which the modified text no longer does. This aligns with the Omission Legal Contradiction definition, where removing essential information creates legal loopholes.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed', the Disclosing party has no guarantee that the Receiving Party has actually destroyed the documents. Furthermore, the omission of the 'archival copy' clause removes an option for the receiving party. These issues are particularly troublesome because the document refers to potential patent acquisition opportunities. In the absence of proof of destruction of data, or an archival copy for reference, Delaware's Trade Secret law could be more easily violated.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware's Trade Secret law requires a proof of destruction or reference for dispute, both now weakened by this deletion. Omitting the clear statement that destruction can be confirmed, or alternatively an archival copy may exist, reduces the burden on the plaintiff, and facilitates compensation requirements under trade secret law, even if that was not the intent of the parties.

perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for the Receiving Party to provide a letter confirming the destruction of Confidential Information, which weakens the Disclosing Party's ability to enforce trade secret protection. While the snippet doesn't explicitly mandate a confirmation letter, it defines 'Misappropriation' as disclosure or use of a trade secret without consent or through improper means (Delaware Code Online Title 6 Â§ 2001). The omission increases the risk of misappropriation by making it more difficult to verify compliance with the confidentiality obligations. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation both indicate that the change makes it more difficult to prove that destruction took place. This omission creates a legal contradiction as it undermines the trade secret protection that is established in the original contract by removing a requirement that is intended to guarantee data security and prevent illicit use or disclosure.
